data.text: I’m checking the bead scope against the local code and the DDx rules first, then I’ll decide whether this is implementable here or whether it has to be rejected as too large for the current depth cap.

data.text: I’ve confirmed the current backend is still the file-backed emulation the bead describes. Next I’m checking whether the generated Axon client and the surrounding package plumbing are already present; if not, this becomes a split/reject case rather than an implementation pass.

data.text: The repo does have a scaffolded `internal/bead/axon` client package, but the backend still persists to local files. I’m now checking whether there’s any existing transport wiring or store-level hook that would let this be switched over without inventing new integration plumbing from scratch.

data.text: This is not a safe implementation pass in the current worktree: the bead spans the backend, generated client integration, store plumbing, and the full axon test matrix, and the instructions explicitly cap decomposition at this depth. I’m writing the required no-change rationale now instead of forcing a partial or speculative change.

Wrote the required no-change rationale at [.ddx/executions/20260505T164659-abc25e07/no_changes_rationale.txt](/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-9c5bca8f-20260505T164659-abc25e07/.ddx/executions/20260505T164659-abc25e07/no_changes_rationale.txt).

The bead was rejected as too large for the current execution depth and still depends on unresolved upstream Axon work, so I did not make a partial implementation or commit anything.
